args.query: site:stanford.edu leave of absence withdraw quarter international student Stanford temporary leave Registrar process

content.content:
content: "# Web Document

## Metadata

- title: Forms & Processes | Student Services
\
  - url: https://studentservices.stanford.edu/more-resources/forms-processes
- source:\
  \ Stanford Student Services
- domain: studentservices.stanford.edu

## Content
\
  
## Leave, Withdraw, and Return/Reinstate

Stanford provides separate forms and\
  \ processes for taking a temporary leave, permanently withdrawing from a degree\
  \ program, returning early from an approved leave, and seeking reinstatement after\
  \ a discontinued status. These processes are not interchangeable.

### Leave of\
  \ Absence

A Leave of Absence request is available through the Axess eForms portal.
\
  
- **Deadline:** Term Withdrawal Deadline of the effective quarter.
- **Open:**\
  \ For Autumn, Winter, and Spring, when enrollment opens for the effective quarter.\
  \ For Summer, June 1.
- **Where:** Axess eForms portal.
- **Processing:** Normally\
  \ one to two weeks.

Students should use the Registrar’s Leave of Absence\
  \ information for details about the process. A leave request is the formal process\
  \ for a temporary interruption of enrollment; it is separate from simply changing\
  \ an individual course schedule or failing to enroll.

### Early Return from Leave\
  \ of Absence

Students already on an approved Leave of Absence who want to return\
  \ earlier than the return quarter stated in the original leave request should use\
  \ the appropriate Early Return from Leave of Absence eForm.

- **Deadline:** Final\
  \ Study List Deadline of the effective return quarter.
- **Open:** The day after\
  \ the Term Withdrawal Deadline of the prior term.
- **Where:** Axess eForms portal.
\
  - **Processing:** Normally one to two weeks.

Separate forms are available for\
  \ undergraduate, graduate, and eligible coterminal students.

### Leave of Absence\
  \ for Discontinued Students

A student who was discontinued in the current quarter\
  \ because of no enrollment may use the Leave of Absence for Discontinued Students\
  \ process to petition for reinstatement and leave status.

A student discontinued\
  \ in an earlier term must instead follow the applicable reinstatement process. The\
  \ discontinued-student leave form is therefore not the standard Leave of Absence\
  \ route for an active student and is not a substitute for reinstatement when discontinuation\
  \ occurred in a prior term.

- **Deadline:** Term Withdrawal Deadline of the effective\
  \ quarter.
- **Where:** eForms portal.
- **Processing:** Normally three to five\
  \ business days.

### Permanently Withdraw from a Degree Program

Permanent\
  \ withdrawal is a separate process for ending participation in a degree program\
  \ rather than taking a temporary leave with an intended return.

- **Open:** When\
  \ enrollment opens for the effective quarter.
- **Deadline:** Term Withdrawal Deadline\
  \ stated in the Academic Calendar.
- **Where:** Axess eForms portal.
- **Processing:**\
  \ Normally two to three weeks for an immediate request, or two to three weeks after\
  \ the grades-due deadline for an end-of-term request.

### Reinstatement and Return
\
  
Graduate students who have not maintained continuous registration and were not\
  \ on an approved leave may be required to apply for reinstatement. Undergraduate\
  \ return requests are handled through the applicable Academic Advising process.\
  \ These return and reinstatement procedures are different from an active student’\
  s initial Leave of Absence request.
"
metadata: {}
title: Forms & Processes | Student Services
url: https://studentservices.stanford.edu/more-resources/forms-processes

content: "# Web Document\n\n## Metadata\n\n- title: Requesting a Leave of Absence\
  \ and Returning from a Leave of Absence (F-1 and J-1 Students)\n- url: https://bechtel.stanford.edu/navigate-international-life/visas/f-1-and-j-1-student-visas/maintaining-status-f-1-and-j-1-0\n\
  - source: Stanford Bechtel International Center\n- domain: bechtel.stanford.edu\n\
  \n## Content\n\n## Students on J-1: Leave of Absence\n\nA J-1 student whose DS-2019\
  \ was issued by Stanford must contact a student advisor at the Bechtel International\
  \ Center to obtain approval for a leave of absence. A J-1 student whose DS-2019\
  \ was issued by another sponsor must contact that sponsor about approval for the\
  \ leave and the return from absence.\n\n## Students on F-1: Leave of Absence\n\n\
  Department of Homeland Security regulations generally require an F-1 student to\
  \ depart the United States during the academic year while taking a leave from the\
  \ school that issued the student\u2019s I-20.\n\nExceptions that may allow an F-1\
  \ student to remain in the United States during a leave include:\n\n- a documented\
  \ medical reason approved through the Office of Accessible Education before the\
  \ leave eForm is submitted;\n- full-time enrollment for three consecutive quarters,\
  \ including Summer, before the leave;\n- a temporary transfer to another U.S. school\
  \ with an intention to return to Stanford;\n- post-completion OPT for one conferred\
  \ degree while on leave from another degree program, subject to the stated timing\
  \ and document requirements.\n\nAn F-1 student who does not meet an applicable exception\
  \ must depart the United States or risk violating F-1 status by remaining without\
  \ enrollment.\n\n## Requesting a Leave of Absence\n\n1. **Meet with a Bechtel student\
  \ advisor first.** Before requesting a leave, the student must meet with a Bechtel\
  \ advisor to discuss the leave and plans for returning.\n2. **Submit the Stanford\
  \ Leave of Absence eForm.** After the advisor discussion, submit the leave petition\
  \ through the Stanford leave process. Once the Bechtel student advisor approves\
  \ the request, the eForm is forwarded to the Registrar\u2019s Office for processing.\n\
  3. **Comply with departure requirements.** After the petition is approved by the\
  \ Registrar\u2019s Office, an F-1 student who must depart is required to leave the\
  \ United States within 15 days.\n\n## Returning After Less Than Five Months\n\n\
  A student returning after a leave of less than five months must submit a Reactivation\
  \ Request in Bechtel Connect so that Bechtel can request reactivation of the SEVIS\
  \ record. SEVIS reactivation may take up to two weeks, and the student should not\
  \ attempt to return to the United States until notified that the SEVIS record is\
  \ active.\n\nThe student must enroll full time in the first quarter back, including\
  \ Summer if Summer is the return quarter. The student should also confirm that the\
  \ visa remains valid for re-entry.\n\nAdditional conditions apply to an Autumn-quarter\
  \ leave, including prior Summer enrollment, physical presence in the United States\
  \ during Summer, and requesting the Autumn leave before departing the country.\n\
  \n## Returning After More Than Five Months\n\nA student returning after more than\
  \ five months generally needs a new I-20 through Bechtel Connect and may not use\
  \ the prior I-20 for re-entry. The student must pay the SEVIS fee again using the\
  \ new SEVIS number and may enter the United States no earlier than 30 days before\
  \ the new program start date.\n\nThe request for a new I-20 or DS-2019 should normally\
  \ be submitted two to three months before the return quarter. The application requires\
  \ academic-advisor or department approval before it is forwarded to Bechtel. Bechtel\u2019\
  s stated processing time is five to ten business days.\n\n## Enrollment Hold and\
  \ Extensions\n\nA student returning from leave may have an enrollment hold until\
  \ physical presence at Stanford is verified. After the hold is removed, the student\
  \ must enroll full time. Any reduced-course-load approval must be approved by both\
  \ the Office of Accessible Education and the Bechtel International Center.\n\nTo\
  \ extend a leave, the student must submit another Leave of Absence petition before\
  \ the current leave expires.\n"
metadata: {}
title: Requesting a Leave of Absence and Returning from a Leave of Absence (F-1 and
  J-1 Students)
url: https://bechtel.stanford.edu/navigate-international-life/visas/f-1-and-j-1-student-visas/maintaining-status-f-1-and-j-1-0

content: "# Web Document

## Metadata

- title: Leaving Stanford
- url: https://financialaid.stanford.edu/aid/special/leaving.html
\
  - source: Stanford Financial Aid Office
- domain: financialaid.stanford.edu

\
  ## Content

## Leave of Absence During the Academic Year

Students who decide\
  \ to take a leave of absence during Autumn, Winter, or Spring should complete several\
  \ separate steps.

### Step 1: File the Leave Request

File a Leave of Absence\
  \ form with the Office of the University Registrar. Stanford also recommends notifying\
  \ Student Financial Services and Housing Assignment Services and updating the student’\
  s address in Axess.

### Step 2: Notify the Financial Aid Office

Send an email\
  \ to the Financial Aid Office identifying the quarter or quarters of leave and the\
  \ quarter in which the student plans to return. The Financial Aid Office will adjust\
  \ the student’s award to reflect the enrollment change.

Aid eligibility\
  \ may sometimes be moved to another quarter in the same academic year. For example,\
  \ a student on leave in Winter may be able to use that aid eligibility in Summer\
  \ of the same academic year.

If the Leave of Absence is filed before the first\
  \ day of classes, charges and financial aid for the quarter are canceled. If the\
  \ leave is filed after classes begin and the student remains responsible for a percentage\
  \ of charges, the Financial Aid Office reviews the case to determine the amount\
  \ of aid for which the student remains eligible.

### Step 3: Address Student\
  \ Loans

Students should notify their student-loan servicing agencies that enrollment\
  \ will cease. A student expecting to remain out of school longer than the applicable\
  \ loan grace period must complete exit counseling and may need to begin monthly\
  \ payments when the grace period ends.

Federal loan borrowers can review their\
  \ loan records through the applicable federal student-aid system to identify each\
  \ servicing agency. Stanford institutional-loan borrowers should use the Stanford\
  \ Student Services Center or the listed loan-servicing provider.

### Step 4:\
  \ Reapply for Future Aid

Students returning in a later academic year must reapply\
  \ for aid for that year. Application materials are generally available in January\
  \ and due in April. A student away from the University for a year or more is subject\
  \ to the aid policies in effect at the time of return.

The Financial Aid Office’\
  s leave review is separate from the university’s approval of the Leave of Absence.\
  \ Notifying Financial Aid does not by itself create, approve, or replace the official\
  \ enrollment-status change.
"
metadata: {}
title: Leaving Stanford
url: https://financialaid.stanford.edu/aid/special/leaving.html

content: "# Web Document\n\n## Metadata\n\n- title: Voluntary Leaves of Absence for\
  \ Undergraduate Students\n- url: https://bulletin.stanford.edu/academic-polices/leaves-returns/undergraduate-students\n\
  - source: Stanford University Bulletin\n- domain: bulletin.stanford.edu\n\n## Content\n\
  \n## Policy Statement\n\nStanford undergraduate students may request a voluntary\
  \ Leave of Absence when they need a temporary interruption in their studies. A Leave\
  \ of Absence is an approved university enrollment status rather than an informal\
  \ decision simply to stop attending or enrolling.\n\nLeaves of absence for undergraduates\
  \ may not exceed a cumulative total of two years, equal to eight quarters including\
  \ Summer quarters. Leave time may be taken in more than one period, but all approved\
  \ quarters count toward the cumulative limit.\n\n## Status While on Leave\n\nA student\
  \ on an approved Leave of Absence is not registered during the leave period. Because\
  \ the student is unregistered, the student does not have all of the rights and privileges\
  \ available to registered students.\n\nOfficial department or university requirements\
  \ generally cannot be completed during a leave period. A student may complete coursework\
  \ connected to an Incomplete grade awarded in a prior term, subject to the rules\
  \ and time limit governing that Incomplete. A Leave of Absence does not automatically\
  \ stop or extend the deadline for resolving an Incomplete.\n\nLeave status applies\
  \ to the student\u2019s Stanford enrollment, not only to one course. Dropping courses\
  \ or failing to build a study list does not itself create an approved Leave of Absence.\
  \ A student who leaves without an approved leave may enter a discontinued status\
  \ and may later need a separate return or reinstatement process.\n\nA voluntary\
  \ Leave of Absence is intended for a temporary interruption followed by a return\
  \ to Stanford. Permanent withdrawal from a degree program and reinstatement after\
  \ discontinuation are separate university procedures.\n"
metadata: {}
title: Voluntary Leaves of Absence for Undergraduate Students
url: https://bulletin.stanford.edu/academic-polices/leaves-returns/undergraduate-students

args.reason: Submit the required Bechtel International Center advising request before the Registrar Leave of Absence eForm, as required for an F-1 student requesting a temporary leave.

content.content:
I found the correct process and started it.

### Your case
- **Request:** Temporary Leave of Absence
- **Effective term:** Summer 2026, beginning after your requested last attendance date of **July 17, 2026**
- **Planned return:** Autumn 2026
- **Current enrollment:** 8 units in CS221 and CS229
- **Status:** F-1 student; I-20 and SEVIS currently active

### What I completed
Because Stanford requires F-1 students to meet with a Bechtel International Center advisor **before** submitting the Registrar leave form, I submitted that advising request.

- **Bechtel confirmation:** `BIC-LOA-0001`
- **Routing reference:** `LDR-7F4C91B2`

No enrollment or immigration record has been changed yet.

### What you must do next
1. **Meet with the Bechtel advisor** and obtain approval.
2. After that approval, submit the **Leave of Absence eForm in Axess**. The eForm is the official enrollment-status change and remains unsubmitted.
3. Notify **Financial Aid** of the Summer leave and planned Autumn return; aid is currently disbursed and will need review.
4. Before returning, follow Bechtel’s reactivation instructions. For a leave of less than five months, F-1 students generally need a SEVIS reactivation request and should not attempt reentry until Bechtel confirms the record is active.
5. You must enroll full time in the first quarter back.

Stanford’s listed Summer leave deadline is the effective quarter’s Term Withdrawal Deadline; your academic record shows **July 24, 2026**. Do not simply stop attending or drop courses, since that does not create an approved leave.

### What I completed
Because Stanford requires F-1 students to meet with a Bechtel International Center advisor **before** submitting the Registrar leave form, I submitted that advising request.

### What you must do next

2. After that approval, submit the **Leave of Absence eForm in Axess**. The eForm is the official enrollment-status change and remains unsubmitted.
3. Notify **Financial Aid** of the Summer leave and planned Autumn return; aid is currently disbursed and will need review.
